title: Scholars bar is closing after 15 years. A new concept is already planned.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Scholars American Bistro & Cocktail Lounge is closing after 15 years at Downtown Crossing on School Street.
The space will reopen in late summer as Off The Rails Boston, focusing on live music and entertainment.
The new venue will feature bands and Americana-inspired food, drawing influences from Nashville and Boston.

title: How much are you spending to attend the World Cup?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston to host seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches starting June 13 at Gillette Stadium, temporarily renamed Boston Stadium.
Commuter Rail round-trip tickets from South Station to Foxborough cost $80 on match days, sparking strong fan reactions.
World Cup attendance costs include escalating prices for tickets, transport, lodging in Boston, and match-day parking at $175.

title: A first look at the massive outdoor bar coming to Cape Cod this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Foley family will open The Backyard by Foley's in Dennis Port, Cape Cod's largest outdoor family-friendly venue.
The 33,650 square-foot space will include a 5,000 square-foot sail-covered main bar and various seating options.
The venue will host rotating food trucks, lawn games, live music, and collaborate with local businesses for events.

title: Chelsea man pleads not guilty after allegedly threatening BU campus, students, deans
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Maximillien Sajous pleaded not guilty to charges including stalking and bomb threats at Boston University.
Sajous allegedly made threats, shared photos of shell casings and a dean's residence online.
He is held without bail due to dangerousness after multiple warrants were issued for his arrest.

title: Healey refuses to alter RMV policy that denies undercover license plates to ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey maintains RMV policy denying confidential plates to most ICE vehicles, citing public safety concerns.
DOJ Assistant Attorney General Brett Shumate calls RMV's policy unconstitutional, threatening legal action if not changed by May 22.
RMV policy allows confidential plates only for active criminal investigations, affecting ICE's Homeland Security Investigations vehicles.

title: Body of Quincy kayaker pulled from Boston Harbor
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: 72-year-old Tri Trinh was found dead in Boston Harbor after setting out in a kayak from Quincy.
The body was discovered near Thompson Island and taken to Beth Israel Deaconess Hospital – Milton for autopsy.
Suffolk County DA reported no obvious signs of trauma or foul play; autopsy results are pending.

title: South Shore school employee pleads guilty to stealing food, equipment for ‘Snack Shack’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Van Cott, 64, pleaded guilty to stealing food and equipment from Plymouth Public Schools for his 'Snack Shack'.
Van Cott admitted using school and USDA funds to purchase restaurant supplies including refrigerators, a freezer, and burger patties.
Sentencing for Van Cott is set for August 19, with potential penalties including up to 20 years in prison and fines.

title: New Allston hotel wins design award
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Atlas Hotel in Allston won the Lifestyle Hotel Public Space category at the 22nd Annual Hospitality Design Awards.
Located near Harvard's Enterprise Research Campus, the hotel features spaces inspired by Ray Oldenburg's 'third places' concept.
Additional amenities include a fitness center, meeting spaces, restaurant Ama, and Foxglove Terrace, a panoramic rooftop bar.

title: JetBlue is ending service at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue will end service at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport on July 8, 2026, citing a capacity crisis.
JetBlue began flights at Manchester-Boston Regional Airport in January 2025, lasting less than two years.
Impacted customers can choose alternative JetBlue flights or receive a full refund, according to JetBlue.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about warmer temperatures into next week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Temperatures in Boston will rise from the 50s today to the 70s and 80s over the weekend.
Meteorologists predict a heatwave next week with Tuesday reaching highs near 90 degrees in Boston.
The warmer weather is due to a jet stream over the east, creating summer-like conditions early next week.
